description: Implement backend unit tests per the approved testing strategy RFC (docs/testing-strategy-rfc.md). Target: service/weather_test.go, service/calendar_test.go, service/news_test.go, service/stocks_test.go, service/cache_test.go. Cover: wmoToCondition table-driven test, aqiCategory all branches, formatDuration, relativeTime, filterToday (all-day, timed, cancelled, wrong-day), AddSymbol/RemoveSymbol sentinel errors. Use in-memory SQLite (:memory: DSN) for DB-backed service tests. Make CacheService an interface or nil-guard cache.Delete before writing stocks tests. Run with go test -race ./... — all must pass.
title: Phase 1: Backend unit tests for pure logic (weather, calendar, stocks, cache)

close_reason: PR #34 merged
description: AssignLabel repository method uses 'ON CONFLICT(task_id, label_id) DO UPDATE' but the unique constraint on task_label_assignments is a partial unique index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) created separately, not an inline UNIQUE constraint in CREATE TABLE. SQLite's ON CONFLICT (columns) clause only recognizes PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraints defined in the table definition, not standalone CREATE UNIQUE INDEX. Result: every POST /api/tasks/{id}/labels returns 500. Fix: replace ON CONFLICT upsert with plain INSERT — service layer already guards against duplicates.
title: Fix AssignLabel SQL: ON CONFLICT clause incompatible with partial unique index

description: LOW finding from PR #28 security review. The task_label_assignments join table has no DB-level constraint preventing a label from one user being assigned to a task owned by a different user. The service layer MUST verify that the task.user_id == label.user_id before creating an assignment. Document this requirement in the task labels service when implementing the app code (phase 2 of bd-1ek).
title: Enforce cross-user label assignment protection in service layer

description: When per-user calendar fetching is wired up, the shared http.Client follows up to 10 redirects with no IP restrictions. An attacker could bypass URL validation via redirects to internal IPs (DNS rebinding). Create a dedicated http.Client with: (1) CheckRedirect that validates redirect target schemes, (2) custom Transport.DialContext that resolves DNS and rejects private/loopback/link-local IPs before connecting. Discovered during security review of PR #38.
title: Add SSRF-safe HTTP transport for user-supplied URL fetches

description: PR #25 review MEDIUM: On tablet 2-col grid, Row 1 renders 3x span=1 skeleton cards which forces the third skeleton to wrap to its own row alone — visually unbalanced during loading. Fix by conditionally rendering 2 or 4 skeletons on tablet so the grid fills evenly. File: frontend/src/components/Dashboard.tsx:253. Discovered during mobile-responsive-bqy PR review.
title: Fix tablet skeleton imbalance in Dashboard.tsx

description: PR #26 review LOW: cors middleware does not emit Vary: Origin. If middleware is ever extended to support dynamic/multiple origins, CDN or proxy caches could serve wrong CORS responses. Zero cost to add proactively. Add w.Header().Add("Vary", "Origin") before setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin. File: backend/internal/middleware/cors.go. Discovered during infra-hardening-9cq PR review.
title: Add Vary: Origin header to CORS middleware

close_reason: news_categories TEXT column replaced entirely by news_category_types lookup + user_news_categories join tables — no CHECK constraint needed
description: LOW finding from PR #29 code review. calendar_ics_url and timezone columns have no length or emptiness constraints. A client could store an empty string or an extremely long value. Add CHECK constraints: calendar_ics_url TEXT CHECK(calendar_ics_url IS NULL OR length(trim(calendar_ics_url)) > 0), timezone TEXT CHECK(timezone IS NULL OR length(trim(timezone)) > 0). Requires a new migration after 007 lands.
title: Add length/emptiness CHECK constraints to user_settings nullable text columns

description: PR #25 review LOW: Current breakpoints are mobile<768, tablet 768-1023, desktop>=1024. iPad Pro landscape (1024px) and iPad Air landscape (1180px) both hit the desktop breakpoint, so the 3-col desktop layout shows on most tablet landscape viewports. Consider raising the tablet->desktop threshold to 1200px so more landscape tablet sizes get the 2-col tablet layout. Or document that 768/1024 matches Tailwind sm/lg defaults intentionally. File: frontend/src/hooks/useWindowSize.ts:12-14. Discovered during mobile-responsive-bqy PR review.
title: Raise tablet breakpoint from 1024px to 1200px in useWindowSize

In production an operator must set both FRONTEND_URL and CORS_ORIGIN to agree, or CORS will silently reject the frontend. Consider defaulting CORSOrigin to FrontendURL in config.Load() when CORS_ORIGIN is unset, so operators only have one URL to configure. File: backend/internal/config/config.go. close_reason: PR #37 merged — AES-256-GCM encryption for sensitive fields with required ENCRYPTION_KEY
description: HIGH security finding from PR #29 review. The calendar_ics_url column stores ICS feed URLs which frequently contain long-lived auth tokens (Google Calendar private feed URLs, Outlook tokens). These are effectively bearer credentials and must NOT be stored in plaintext. Implement an EncryptionService in backend/internal/service/encryption.go with: Encrypt(plaintext string) (ciphertext string, error) and Decrypt(ciphertext string) (plaintext string, error) using AES-GCM with a key derived from a new ENCRYPTION_KEY env var. Inject into the user settings service. Encrypt on write, decrypt on read. Update .env.example and README with the new env var.
title: Implement EncryptionService for calendar_ics_url in user_settings

description: PR #25 review MEDIUM: typeof window \!== 'undefined' check inside useState lazy initializer is unnecessary in a Vite CSR app (no SSR). The fallback 1280 default would mask real bugs if SSR were ever added. Remove guards; use window.innerWidth/innerHeight directly in initializer. File: frontend/src/hooks/useWindowSize.ts:18-21. Discovered during mobile-responsive-bqy PR review.
title: Remove redundant SSR guard in useWindowSize.ts

description:
LOW findings that can ship together in one PR:

1. AddSymbol handler (stocks.go): Returns 400 'invalid symbol' for ALL errors including DB failures. DB errors should return 500 'internal server error'. Define ErrSymbolValidation sentinel in service layer (matching ErrTaskValidation pattern) and use errors.Is to distinguish 400 vs 500.

2. Unchecked json.NewEncoder(w).Encode() return values across all handlers (tasks.go, stocks.go, dashboard.go, and other handlers). Add error logging: if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v); err != nil { slog.Warn("encode response", "error", err) }. Note: this is a project-wide pattern — fix all handlers in one sweep.

3. Timestamp precision mismatch: Go timeFormat constant uses 3 decimal places (milliseconds) but SQLite strftime uses 6 (microseconds). Change Go format to "2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000Z" (6 decimals) in sqlite_task_repository.go and sqlite_stocks_watchlist_repository.go to match SQLite trigger/UPSERT output.
title: HTTP handler error handling cleanup

close_reason: Fixed in PR #36 — SettingsPanel validates parseFloat with isNaN guard
description: handleSave passes parseFloat(latitude)/parseFloat(longitude) directly to the API. If the user types a non-numeric string (e.g. 'abc') parseFloat returns NaN and the backend receives an invalid float. Add a NaN guard: if (isNaN(parsed)) treat it as null (same as empty string). File: frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.tsx. Discovered during review of PR #33 (bd-d1h).
title: Guard parseFloat against NaN in SettingsModal handleSave

description: LOW finding from PR #27 code review. DragHandle uses useState(false) + onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave to toggle hover styles, causing a React re-render on each hover event. title: Refactor DragHandle hover state from useState to CSS class

close_reason: PR #24 merged
description:
M3+M4+M5: Three small hardening fixes across the service and repository layers, all shippable in one PR.

M3 (service/stocks.go:193): Symbol not URL-encoded in Finnhub quote URL. Change:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", symbol)
to:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", url.QueryEscape(symbol))
SearchSymbols already does this correctly — apply the same pattern to fetchFinnhub.

M4 (service/news.go:92): io.ReadAll on upstream response body with no size limit. Change to:
  io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 1<<20)) — consistent with stocks service.

M5 (repository/sqlite_task_repository.go:95): sql.ErrNoRows compared with == instead of errors.Is. Change to errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) for future-proofing against wrapped errors.
title: Service layer hardening: url.QueryEscape, io.LimitReader, errors.Is

close_reason: Merged PR #9

description: PR #24 review MEDIUM: category and apiKey interpolated directly into GNews URL via fmt.Sprintf. Should use url.Values to encode all query params. If apiKey ever contains URL-special chars the URL breaks. Change to: params := url.Values{}; params.Set("category", category); params.Set("apikey", s.apiKey); ... url + "?" + params.Encode(). Discovered during service-hardening-9ya PR review.
title: GNews URL params not encoded with url.Values (news.go:78-81)

description: LOW finding from PR #28 code review. The color column has no format validation — a label color could be any string. Requires a new migration to ALTER or recreate the table after 006 lands.

close_reason: Completed: 8-phase backend layer boundaries refactor merged in PR #40
description:
Create shared internal packages to eliminate boilerplate and inconsistency across handlers and services:
1. internal/response/ package — WriteJSON(w, status, v) and WriteError(w, status, msg) helpers to replace repeated json.NewEncoder patterns and silent encode-error discards across all handlers
2. internal/httpclient/ or service-level helper — shared pattern for external API calls (set headers, io.LimitReader body, unmarshal JSON) currently duplicated across weather, news, stocks, commute, sunrise, quotes services
3. Centralize sentinel errors — ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidInput etc. in a shared package so handlers can use errors.Is() consistently
4. Shared validator instance — single go-playground/validator/v10 instance in internal/validate/ or similar, reused across all handlers
Goal: reduce per-handler boilerplate, ensure consistent HTTP status codes and response format across all endpoints.
title: Harden Go backend HTTP layer

description: assignLabel only has onSettled (invalidate), unlike removeLabel which has full optimistic cancel/snapshot/rollback. Add onMutate optimistic update so the label chip appears instantly when assigned. Mirror the pattern from removeLabel. Discovered during review of PR #32 (bd-1ek).
title: Add optimistic update to assignLabel mutation in useLabels.ts

close_reason: PR #39 merged — CORS_ORIGIN validation + Vary: Origin header + null rejection + path normalization
description: PR #26 review MEDIUM (security): cors middleware always emits Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. If CORS_ORIGIN is set to * the browser rejects it, but the server still emits the unsafe header pair — a misconfiguration footgun. Add startup validation in config.Load() or main.go that errors/panics if CORSOrigin is '*' or empty after trimming, consistent with how SESSION_SECRET is validated at startup. File: backend/internal/middleware/cors.go:14, backend/internal/config/config.go. Discovered during infra-hardening-9cq PR review.
title: Add startup validation: reject CORS_ORIGIN=* when Credentials: true

description: Refactor the per-handler userIDFromRequest() helper into a shared WithUserID middleware chained after requireAuth. The middleware extracts sess.UserID and stores it on the context using a typed key. Handlers then call middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context()) directly, removing duplicated session-extraction logic from tasks.go and stocks.go. See conversation for full implementation sketch.
title: Add middleware to extract userID from session onto request context

Add validation in the user settings service or calendar service: only allow https:// (and optionally http://) schemes before fetching. Return a 422 if the scheme is disallowed. File: backend/internal/service/user_settings.go or calendar.go. Discovered during security review of PR #33 (bd-d1h).
title: Add SSRF scheme validation for calendar_ics_url on backend

close_reason: PR merged
description: Label picker dropdown rows have no hover state, making it hard to see which label you're hovering over. Add a subtle label-color-tinted background on hover for clear visual feedback.
title: Add hover effect to label picker dropdown items

close_reason: PR #35 merged
description: Label picker dropdown opens correctly but clicking label items to assign them does nothing. Root causes: (1) mousedown outside-click handler may close picker during concurrent re-renders since pickerRef.current.contains() could fail; (2) no optimistic update on assignLabel means zero immediate visual feedback. Fix: add onMouseDown stopPropagation on dropdown div, add zIndex to open task row, add optimistic update to assignLabel.
title: Fix label picker: clicks not registering on dropdown items

description: useLabels.ts exports an updateLabel mutation that is never imported anywhere in the frontend. Remove it to avoid dead-code confusion. Discovered during review of PR #32 (bd-1ek).
title: Remove dead updateLabel export from useLabels.ts

close_reason: Fixed in PR #22 — repo Update/Delete now returns (int64, error), service checks rowsAffected == 0
description:
The repository Update and Delete methods currently return ErrTaskNotFound / ErrSymbolNotFound when rows affected == 0. This is business logic that belongs in the service layer, not the data layer.

Fix:
1. sqlite_task_repository.go Update and Delete: change return signature to (rowsAffected int64, err error) instead of (TaskRow, error) / error. Remove the rows==0 check and ErrTaskNotFound return.
2. sqlite_stocks_watchlist_repository.go Remove: change to return (rowsAffected int64, err error). Remove the rows==0 ErrSymbolNotFound check.
3. Update TaskRepository and StocksWatchlistRepository interface signatures to match.
4. In service/tasks.go Update and Delete: check rowsAffected == 0 and return ErrTaskNotFound.
5. In service/stocks.go RemoveSymbol: check rowsAffected == 0 and return ErrSymbolNotFound.

Repository Get (single row) may still surface sql.ErrNoRows — the service wraps it into a domain error.
title: Fix layer responsibility violation: move rows-affected not-found checks from repository to service

close_reason: Tasks migration (003_task_priorities, 004_tasks) merged in PR #11
description: Tasks are stored in-memory only and lost on every server restart. Implement file-based or SQLite persistence so tasks survive restarts. SQLite preferred — single file, no external deps, supports CRUD well. Add migration on startup.
title: Persist tasks to disk (SQLite or JSON file)

close_reason: Merged via PR #17
description: Currently all auth redirects use window.location.href which causes full page reloads, destroying React state and TanStack Query cache. Adopt react-router-dom with useNavigate() and <Navigate /> so auth transitions are client-side. Affects: App.tsx, ProtectedRoute.tsx, LoginPage.tsx.
title: Add React Router for SPA navigation (replace window.location.href)

description: PR #26 review LOW: corsOrigin() in config.go accepts any non-empty string including trailing slashes or whitespace. A value like 'http://example.com/' (trailing slash) silently breaks CORS in browsers. Add strings.TrimRight(strings.TrimSpace(s), '/') or validate with url.Parse before accepting. File: backend/internal/config/config.go:114-119. Discovered during infra-hardening-9cq PR review.
title: Trim and validate CORS_ORIGIN value at config load time

description: PR #25 review LOW: window resize fires on every pixel during drag. The resize listener in useWindowSize re-renders on each event. Since layout only changes at 768/1024px boundaries, add a guard: only call setSize when the breakpoint actually changes (compare getBreakpoint(w) !== size.breakpoint). Alternatively, debounce the handler with a 50-100ms delay. File: frontend/src/hooks/useWindowSize.ts. Discovered during mobile-responsive-bqy PR review.
title: Add breakpoint-change guard to useWindowSize resize handler (debounce)

description: Create a logging middleware that builds a slog.Logger with request-scoped attributes (user_id, request_id, method, path) pre-attached via slog.With() and stores it on the request context. Add LoggerFromContext(ctx) helper to retrieve it. Update all handlers and services to use LoggerFromContext instead of slog.Default() directly, so every log line in a request chain automatically includes the user context. Chain this middleware after WithUserID so user_id is available when the logger is constructed.
title: Add context-aware slog logger middleware for per-request structured logging

close_reason: Implemented backend unit and integration tests: 35 tests across service (cache, quotes, stocks, tasks, weather, sunrise) and handler (tasks CRUD, dashboard fan-out) layers. All pass with go test -race ./...
description: Zero test files exist in the backend. Add unit tests for each service (mock HTTP client), integration tests for handlers, and a test for the dashboard fan-out. Target: go test -race ./... passes with meaningful coverage.
notes: Won't complete — pivoting to a better testing strategy. Will investigate e2e/component/contract patterns before writing tests. Unit tests only for genuinely complex functions.
title: Add backend unit and integration tests

close_reason: SQLite persistence + per-user scoping implemented
description: Stocks watchlist service is still in-memory. This issue covers two things: (1) Replace the in-memory watchlist with SQLite persistence using the stocks_watchlist table from migration 005. Implement add/remove/list against SQLite in the stocks service so the watchlist survives restarts. (2) Scope all watchlist operations to the authenticated user — add user_id FK to watchlist entries, update service/handlers to filter by the session user. The migrations and OAuth backend are already in place.
title: Per-user stocks watchlist (link watchlist to authenticated user)

close_reason: Implemented symbol search UI in StocksCard with add/remove. Fixed lint error in useSymbolSearch.ts (cascading setState in effect). All endpoints and components already existed and build/lint clean.
description: Backend already has GET /api/stocks/search endpoint but there is no frontend UI to use it. Add a search modal or inline input to StocksCard that lets users search for and add symbols to their watchlist without editing env vars.
title: Add symbol search UI to StocksCard

description: No CI exists. Add .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on push/PR: go build, go vet, go test -race ./..., npm run build, npm run lint. Optional: Docker build check. Should block merges on failure.
title: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline

description: PR #25 review LOW: isTablet is destructured in NewsCard props but not used to drive any inner grid logic — only isMobile does. Either remove the prop (NewsCard doesn't need to know about tablet) or add a comment explaining why isTablet is passed but inner layout stays 2-col at tablet width. File: frontend/src/components/NewsCard.tsx. Discovered during mobile-responsive-bqy PR review.
title: Remove or document unused isTablet prop in NewsCard

close_reason: Testing strategy RFC written at docs/testing-strategy-rfc.md
description:
The initial unit/integration test approach was closed without merging. Need to decide on the right testing pattern before writing any tests.

Consider:
- E2E tests: test the full stack (browser to backend)
- Component testing: test individual frontend components in isolation
- Contract testing: verify API contracts between frontend and backend
- Unit tests: keep only for genuinely complex functions (business logic, parsing)

Deliverables: decision doc with chosen strategy and rationale, example test(s) demonstrating the pattern, and guidelines on when unit tests are appropriate vs the chosen higher-level approach.
title: Investigate and define testing strategy for the project

close_reason: PR #36 opened: move settings into profile dropdown + sliding right panel
description: 1. Nest Settings menu item inside the profile avatar dropdown (where logout is). 2. Convert SettingsModal from full-screen centered modal to a sliding right panel (440px, glass-morphism, overlay). 3. Remove standalone settings gear button from dashboard header. 4. Hybrid save: auto-save for toggles (news categories), manual save for text inputs (location, calendar URL).
title: Move settings into profile dropdown + sliding right panel

close_reason: Google OAuth backend merged in PR #13
description: Implement direct Google OAuth 2.0 / OIDC flow in Go using golang.org/x/oauth2. Endpoints: GET /api/auth/login (redirect to Google), GET /api/auth/callback (exchange code, upsert user record, set signed HTTP-only session cookie), POST /api/auth/logout (clear cookie). Any Google account can sign up (open). Store user in SQLite users table on first login.
title: Implement Google OAuth 2.0 backend

close_reason: Completed — PR #16 merged
description: Add a profile panel/dropdown accessible from the dashboard header showing: (1) User's Google avatar and display name pulled from the session. (2) Sign out button that calls POST /api/auth/logout and redirects to /login. This supersedes the temporary sign-out button added for testing. API keys are managed by the company and are not user-configurable.
title: User profile panel (avatar, display name, sign out)

title: Persist stocks watchlist to disk
